name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash at 10404 Venice Blvd., Culver City, CA 90232 in the Washington Culver neighborhood is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.9 stars across 1118 reviews. Call +1 310-558-8433.
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a storefront lender operating in Memphis, Tennessee, at 5126 Summer Avenue. The company specializes in short-term emergency lending products designed to provide quick access to cash for consumers facing immediate financial needs. Their Memphis location operates extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays) to accommodate working customers, with in-store applications and funding available. The company positions itself as a neighborhood lending solution with personalized service and clear disclosure of loan terms before commitment.

The company offers three primary loan products: payday loans up to $425 with repayment due around the next paycheck, lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw amounts and repayment over time, and title loans up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral. Beyond lending, Speedy Cash provides money services including money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa debit card services. All loans require standard documentation (government ID, proof of income, proof of address, phone and email), with title loans requiring vehicle appraisal and proof of insurance.

Speedy Cash distinguishes itself through in-store consultation where customers can ask questions throughout the application process before committing, in-person document review, and clear upfront pricing. The company maintains an online application option for customer convenience while emphasizing the availability of in-store guidance. They operate multiple Memphis-area locations (at least 8 additional stores listed) and maintain bilingual customer service (English: 1-888-333-1360, Spanish: 1-855-734-0111).

However, these are high-cost short-term loans typical of the payday lending industry. While the website does not disclose APR or finance charges, payday loans historically carry APRs of 200-400%. The $425 payday loan limit is relatively low, and while the company advertises transparency about costs, consumers should carefully review the actual finance charges and total repayment amount before signing. These products are designed as emergency solutions, not sustainable long-term borrowing.
pros: Payday loans available up to $425 with clear repayment timeline (due on next paycheck)
Lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible borrowing and repayment structure
Title loans up to $4,000 for customers with vehicle collateral
Extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday) accommodate working customers
In-store consultation allows customers to review terms and ask questions before signing
Multiple additional services: money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, Green Dot debit cards
Bilingual customer service available (English and Spanish)
Online application option available for initial convenience
Eight additional Memphis-area locations provide neighborhood accessibility
cons: APR and total finance charges not disclosed on website; payday loans typically carry 200-400% APR
Low maximum payday loan amount ($425) limits usefulness for larger emergencies
Title loans require vehicle collateral appraisal and proof of insurance, limiting accessibility
Payday loans require repayment in full within ~2 weeks, creating risk of rollover debt cycles
High-cost debt products not suitable for non-emergency or long-term financial needs
best_for: Consumers with immediate small emergency expenses ($100-$425) and stable paycheck income
Vehicle owners needing moderate emergency funds ($1,000-$4,000) willing to pledge collateral
Customers seeking rapid funding who prefer in-person guidance and document review
Borrowers comfortable with high-cost short-term debt as a genuine emergency solution only
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for consumers facing genuine emergencies who need $100-$4,000 in rapid funding and have either stable payday income (for payday/personal loans) or vehicle collateral (for title loans). The critical caveat is that these are expensive short-term loans with APRs typically exceeding 200%; they should never be used for non-emergency expenses or as a substitute for traditional credit, and borrowers risk entering debt cycles if they cannot repay within the short timeframe.
services: Title loans up to $4,000 (secured by vehicle)
Green Dot Visa debit card services
